Title: Sona Buvelle
Age: CurrentAround 21 years old Timeline BornAround 976 AN Edit

children.title: Early Life
children.textContent: When she was an infant, Sona was left in a monastery in the Ionian province of Galrin. The monks were given no other clue about her origins besides a mysterious stringed instrument that was delivered with her. Even so, these monks were generous and made certain that Sona and the other children under their care lived happy lives. As she grew older, her caretakers later realized that Sona could not speak, but her thoughtful nature brought comfort to the other children, who would frequently smile whenever she was around. As a child, Sona learned how to play the instrument she had been left with. No visiting teachers or musicians had ever seen anything like it before, so she had to teach herself.

children.title: Adoption by House Buvelle
children.textContent:
When Noxus began to land troops in the northern provinces of Ionia, the monastery evacuated the orphans and struck a deal with a Demacian trader, who took Sona and her friends on a ship to Demacia, where they would be cared for by the Illuminators. The Buvelle family, led by Lord Barrett and Lady Lestara, were prominent patrons of the Illuminators, and they gladly obliged when asked to help care for nine war orphans over three months. The Buvelles were amazed by Sona's musical talent and were almost immediately fond of her, and though eight of the orphans would leave, Sona decided to stay.

Over the next few years, Sona became close with her new family, including her adoptive sister Kahina and especially with Lady Lestara. As Sona was unfamiliar with the Demacian language, the Buvelles developed a personalized sign language so they could better communicate. Out of appreciation for her new home, Sona held recitals so she could share her music with the public. She also studied in the libraries of the Illuminators to learn more about her instrument, and came to believe it was an etwahl, a rare magical artifact. Her connection to this instrument meant that Sona was a mage, which her mother urged her to keep secret from the other Demacians, especially the mageseekers.

children.title: Requiem for Xin Zhao
children.textContent: After the death of the Lord Seneschal Xin Zhao in Ionia, King Jarvan III arranged his funeral to be held in the royal gardens, where he was to be honored with a statue carved in the months after his death. In memory of a man she had known since she was young, Sona composed a traditional lamentarion in the Ionian language spoken in Raikkon, performed on her etwahl and accompanied by a choir of Illuminators. When the funeral was over, Sona met with Luxanna Crownguard, who was one of the choir members during the performance.

children.title: Funeral of Lord Barrett
children.textContent:
When Sona was a teenager, her father was slain by Noxian mages during the Battle of the Gates of Mourning. Lord Barrett had been a much-beloved figure among the Demacian people, and the entire kingdom mourned his loss, including King Jarvan III, who had considered him a close friend. Lady Lestara was especially heartbroken by the loss, and Sona and Kahina did their best to comfort her as they all grieved together.

The funeral for Lord Barrett was held three weeks after the battle, with so many mourners in attendance that the Great City was not large enough to hold them all. Many prominent nobles gave eulogies for Sona's father, but she believed none of them described who really he was, save for King Jarvan III. One of the nobles, Lord Eldred of the Mageseekers, even went so far as to misrepresent Barrett as an anti-magic figure, even though he had known his daughter's secret and protected her for years. After the others had spoken, Lestara announced that she was donating the Buvelle residence and their private collection of books to become a public library. At her mother's request, Sona then performed a song she had composed in her father's honor.

Later, after the other guests had left, Kahina disclosed to her mother and sister that she was planning to join the Illuminators as a knight, which meant Sona was now the sole heir to the Buvelle name. Sona was shaken by the news. Lestara worried that as an adopted child, and one who was not of Demacian blood, it could prove difficult for her. Sona also understood that mages would only be further persecuted after the king's best friend had been killed by one, and with Barrett no longer around to protect her she no longer felt safe in Demacia. Sona tried to run away from home late that night. Her mother caught her, but instead of stopping her, Lestara decided to come as well. The two of them set on a journey together back to Ionia, withdrawing from all courtly engagements.

children.title: Return to Ionia
children.textContent: When Sona arrived in the Ionia, it was undergoing a great "restoration" after the war. The First Lands had changed, however, with the people there having endured much in the time she had been gone. At some point, Sona traveled with Ryze to the Hirana Monastery so he could gather a World Rune. While there, she defended him against the Navori Brotherhood, using her etwahl to defend him against their pursuers. After some time in Ionia, Sona decided she no longer belonged there and went back to Demacia with her mother.

children.title: Concert at the Argentine Inn
children.textContent:
Despite Sona's decision to return to Demacia, the problems that had caused her to leave were still very much there. Following the death of King Jarvan III, Demacia found itself in a period of rebellion and civil unrest. The power of the mageseekers had expanded significantly, with many innocents being persecuted as a result.

One day, Sona received a letter from her old friend Etra, who wanted to perform with her at the Argentine Inn. They took the stage with a drummer named Cal, with Etra as the vocalist and Sona at center stage with her etwahl. The audience grew quickly as soon as Sona started to play, and she tried to soothe their collective worries following the king's assassination. However, a group of mageseekers soon made their way into the audience as well, and they attacked Sona after seeing the magical shapes drifting through the air. The audience fled in the chaos, and to protect Etra, Sona fought the mageseekers with her music, forcing them to painfully dance until they were knocked out cold with no memory of the event. With the mageseekers unconscious, Sona fled the scene through the forest.

mainContent.title: Appearance
mainContent.textContent: Sona is a slender, pale-skinned woman with long blue hair and yellow at the ends. Her robes is a long, elegant gown with a distinctive palette of blue, dark blue, turquoise and gold. The most characteristic ornaments of her attire are the golden diadem on her head between her hair. She also has an Etwahl that floats along with her.

mainContent.title: Personality
mainContent.textContent: Sona is a very graceful and elegant young woman. She best (and primarily) expresses herself through her music (although she also as the ability to express her thoughts and communicate with others via telepathic speech), which is best known for it's melodious nature. This highly reflects her gracious and refined personality. Sona is a very caring and gentle woman who helps out others in need and comforts those in need.

children.title: House Buvelle
children.textContent: Sona was adopted by Lord Barrett and Lady Lestara of House Buvelle, and their daughter Kahina became her adoptive sister. She was very close with all of them, especially her mother Lestara. When they learned she was capable of magic, her parents kept it a secret to protect her from the Mageseekers. Sona was deeply upset by the death of her adoptive father, and his absence led her to leave Demacia for a time with her mother. Sona also became the house's sole heir after her sister joined the Illuminators, as their knights were not allowed to marry or hold titles.

children.title: Illuminators
children.textContent: When Sona first came to Demacia, the Illuminators and their monks cared for her and the other orphans until she was adopted by the Buvelles. Her mother Lestara left the order to marry Lord Barrett, and her sister Kahina is a current member. Both of her parents were strong supporters of the organization, and Sona was able to learn about her etwahl and herself by studying in the Illuminators' library.

children.title: Mageseekers
children.textContent: After discovering her etwahl was a magical instrument, Sona's mother urged her to keep it secret so she would not be hunted by the mageseekers. With the death of her father, the mageseekers gained significant power by decree of King Jarvan III. Sona was afraid of Lord Eldred at Barrett's funeral, and his fanatical hatred of people like her was part of the reason she left Demacia. She currently hates and blames the mageseekers as the reason her home no longer feels like home, and despite her gentle nature, she is willing to inflict pain on them to protect her friends.

children.title: Ryze
children.textContent: While she was in Ionia, Sona traveled with Ryze to the Hirana Monastery and fought the Navori Brotherhood who ambushed them. She learned about his hunt for "world-ending relics" and wondered if the runes could truly be that dangerous.

children.title: Sylas
children.textContent: Despite opposing the mageseekers, Sona refused to join Sylas' rebellion, believing he was only creating more problems for mages.

children.title: Jarvan IV
children.textContent: Sona was frustrated by Jarvan IV's early reign, believing he could resolve many of the kingdom's problems with a single decree.

children.title: Irelia
children.textContent: Sona met Irelia during the "restoration" in Ionia, but has not seen her since then.

children.title: Taric
children.textContent: Sona knew who Taric was before his exile from Demacia.

children.title: Fiddlesticks
children.textContent: Sona knows the old songs about Fiddlesticks, but does not believe the demon is actually real.

children.title: Ezreal
children.textContent: Ezreal once tried attend one of Sona's recitals, but was denied entry.
